task_description: Python factorial(n) function with edge cases and validation
verified_by: claude (cloud) — sanity check via 8 numerical/exception tests, all passed
notes: First delegation to granite4.1:8b after model swap from granite-code:8b. 19s likely includes cold-start model load; subsequent delegations should be faster. Code style slightly unconventional (loop iterates n→1 instead of 2→n) but correct and clean.

task_description: Implement 8 spacecraft-thermal Python functions (Gamaunt Eqs. 15-20 + albedo + earth_ir)
verified_by: claude (cloud) review + 10-case numerical sanity check against Gamaunt reference values (all passed)
bugs_caught: eclipse_fraction: (1/180) used instead of (1/math.pi) — Python's math.acos returns radians not degrees
notes: 7 of 8 functions verbatim correct, eclipse_fraction needed two targeted patches. View factor sanity-checked against Gamaunt's 26% Earth view claim — passes. Sanity-checked: ISS eclipse at beta=0 → 39% (Gamaunt 38%); SS eclipse at beta=58.5 → 23.1% (Gamaunt 23%); radiator capacity Gamaunt example → 10.00 W (matches Gamaunt 10 W); SS beta at vernal equinox 6 AM node → 82.4° (expected ~82°). Multi-function batch delegation works for granite4.1:8b. STRUVE thermal estimator notebook v0.1 build, deployed to STRUVE/struve_bus_thermal_estimator.ipynb.

task_description: Swift StatusPanelView.swift — SwiftUI Form with Sections, LabeledContent rows, observed BridgeStatus
verified_by: claude (cloud) — wrote the file directly instead of waiting for and reviewing granite's output
notes: Granite took 2+ minutes on this prompt (more complex Swift with conditional rendering, computed colors, and form structure). Decision: write directly in cloud rather than wait+patch. Granite did eventually respond (4211 chars), consumed but not used. Pattern observation for the tuning file: SwiftUI views with multiple sections, computed properties, and Form/LabeledContent idioms are above granite4.1:8b's comfort zone — round-trip + patch is slower than direct cloud write. Code-generation routing policy refinement: prefer cloud for SwiftUI structural work, delegate granite for AppKit boilerplate, Python physics functions, and well-specified single-function tasks.

task_description: Write Swift MenuBarApp.swift for macOS menu bar app (AppKit + SwiftUI @main)
bugs_caught: missing import AppKit and import SwiftUI (file would not compile)
missing @NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor in the @main struct (AppDelegate never instantiated by SwiftUI)
weak var statusItem: NSStatusItem? — wrong, NSStatusItem must be strongly held by owning object or deallocates immediately
trailing top-level statement `NSApplication.shared.delegate = AppDelegate()` not valid in @main SwiftUI app and creates an instance that immediately drops
menu items missing explicit `target = self` — items attached to NSStatusItem do not find AppDelegate via responder chain
notes: First Swift delegation. Granite produced ~50% of the boilerplate cleanly (selectors, menu construction, image template flag) but missed framework-lifecycle details. New entry for granite4.1.md tuning file: 'When generating AppKit code, do not blindly apply weak references — NSStatusItem, NSWindowController, NSWindow lifecycle requires strong ownership by the responsible object.' Effort reduction estimate: ~50-60% vs writing from scratch. Step 1 of C2G v0.2 app: swiftui_app_v2/MenuBarApp.swift produced and validated by code review (build verification waits on Andrew importing into Xcode).

task_description: Debug missing menu bar icon after Step 4/5 Xcode integration — diagnostic + cloud edits only
notes: Pure macOS platform debugging session — ~2 hours of diagnostic across Info.plist (LSUIElement vs INFOPLIST_KEY_LSUIElement), App Sandbox + entitlements, Xcode Build Phases (Info.plist in Copy Bundle Resources), Launch Services cache, NSApp.setActivationPolicy race conditions, Xcode incremental-build staleness. Resolution path: revert MenuBarApp.swift to the morning's known-good version + Mac restart. The setActivationPolicy line added during diagnosis was itself the bug — it races LSUIElement during applicationDidFinishLaunching and produces a status item with zero window height. Captured four macOS-specific lessons in granite4.1.md (no setActivationPolicy with LSUIElement, GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE coexists with explicit Info.plist, lsregister sometimes insufficient — Mac restart resolves it, stat -f for build-timestamp diagnostic). Bumped skill to 0.3.0 with two new orchestrator-level patterns: 'decision NOT to delegate is itself a data point' and 'remove until it works again beats add diagnostic instrumentation' for SwiftUI/AppKit lifecycle bugs. This is the third consecutive C2G session classified not_invoked — three different task classes (probe debugging, SwiftUI structural work, macOS platform debugging) consistently route to cloud. The not_invoked rate matters for PRD-002 measurement: it tells us which task classes the local model genuinely cannot help with.

task_description: POSIX sh json_num(key,file) — extract numeric JSON field without jq/python
verified_by: claude (cloud) review + unit tests on single-line JSON
bugs_caught: grep anchored on ^ — assumes each field on its own line, fails on single-line {seq:..}
used \s (GNU-only) — silently fails on macOS BSD grep
exit-code check read sed's status not the grep match
notes: Right shape, 3 portability/logic bugs; effectively rewritten in cloud keeping the grep+sed approach (used [[:space:]] and grep -o, no anchor). Part of the bridge_delegate helper build, skill v0.4.0. Bridge liveness confirmed via this delegation (processing.lock in 2s).

task_description: Batch of 2 bash functions: json_str(key,file) string extractor + write_status_json(dir,model,version,pid,seq,state) atomic heartbeat writer
verified_by: claude (cloud) review + integration test via fake watcher + live read by bridge_delegate status
bugs_caught: json_str: unescaped nested double-quotes in grep pattern (broken)
json_str: assumed no space after the colon, but the writer emits quoted values with a space; would not match
notes: write_status_json usable near-verbatim (correct atomic temp+rename, right fields, self-computes epoch) — shipped into start_local_ai.sh v0.2.9. json_str rewritten in cloud, same string-parsing-with-grep weak spot as json_num. 1 of 2 functions verbatim; multi-function batch again viable.

task_description: POSIX sh si_prefix(exp) — map power-of-ten exponent to SI prefix symbol via case
verified_by: claude (cloud) review — case logic correct for all specified exponents
notes: Clean first-pass, verbatim usable. Live end-to-end test of the assembled bridge_delegate helper (send -> poll -> DONE -> auto-ack) against real Ollama. Warm latency ~5s. Simple case-statement mapping is squarely in granite4.1's comfort zone.
